title: Genesis and Vault to Create Australia’s Third-Largest Gold Miner
summary: Genesis Minerals Ltd. and Vault Minerals Ltd. will merge to create Australia’s third-largest gold producer, a day after rival bidder Regis Resources Ltd. said it would not counter Genesis’s bid and pulled out of the running.

title: Canadian Oil Sands Firms Reach Carbon Capture Deal With Alberta
summary: A group of Canadian oil sands producers reached an agreement with the Alberta and federal governments on next steps toward building a carbon capture and storage system.

title: Trump Touts Blockade Fee, 12 States Suing Paramount, More
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 4:55

title: TransDigm Calls Off Deal for Rival After DOJ Lawsuit Threat
summary: Aerospace parts maker TransDigm walked away from its $960 million takeover of rival company Stellant Systems after the US Justice Department threatened to sue to block the deal over antitrust concerns.

title: Crude Oil Extends Advance, Asian Stocks Decline: Markets Wrap
summary: Oil extended gains and Asian stocks dropped after the standoff between the US and Iran intensified, raising concerns that disruptions to energy supplies could accelerate inflation.

title: Oil Extends Surge as Trump Reinstates Blockade on Iranian Ships
summary: Oil extended gains after surging almost 10% on Monday, as US President Donald Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded payment for all other cargo moving through the vital waterway.

title: Venezuela Names Its US Ambassador as Head of Foreign Policy, Trade
summary: Venezuela’s Acting President Delcy Rodríguez merged the government’s foreign policy and international trade ministries into a single office headed by Caracas’s ambassador to the US, Félix Plasencia.

title: Gold Holds Decline on Renewed Hormuz Blockade and Hawkish Fed
summary: Gold held a decline as escalating hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz and remarks by a US monetary policymaker raised the likelihood of interest-rate hikes to contain inflation.

title: Seidler Equity Is Looking to Sell Stake in 139-Year-Old Baseball Gear Maker Rawlings
summary: The private equity owner of Rawlings Sporting Goods Co., one of the oldest makers of baseball apparel and equipment, is exploring the sale of its stake in the company in an effort to tap booming investor demand for sports-related investments.

title: US Hits Iran With Strikes, Blockade as Trump Plans Hormuz Charge
summary: President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement on all other cargo shipped through the waterway.

title: Brazil’s Oncoclinicas Said to File for Out-of-Court Debt Rework
summary: Oncoclinicas do Brasil Servicos Medicos SA is preparing to file for an out-of-court debt restructuring, which could come as soon as Monday night, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Shein’s Slowing Growth Risks Weighing on Hong Kong IPO Valuation
summary: Shein has finally won long-awaited Chinese regulatory approval for its planned Hong Kong listing, but slowing business growth threatens to weigh on the valuation it can command in the initial public offering.

title: India’s Beaten-Down Large Caps Set to Rebound on Foreign Flows
summary: India’s large-cap stocks are set to narrow their underperformance to smaller peers, supported by the return of foreign investors, an improving earnings outlook and attractive valuations, according to strategists.

title: Wells Fargo’s Mike Mayo: Citi My Top Pick of Big Banks
summary: Investors are preparing for a significant earnings week as five of the six largest US banks, including JPMorgan, Citi, Wells Fargo, Bank of America, and Goldman Sachs, are scheduled to report their quarterly results within a few hours of each other. Mike Mayo, Head of US Large-Cap Bank Research at Wells Fargo, joined the program to share his outlook. He expects overall bank earnings to increase by 15 to 20 percent year-over-year, driven by a combination of strong Wall Street trading revenues and robust commercial loan growth on Main Street. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: After Biden Denied Alabama Coal Waste Bid, Trump Reverses Course
summary: The Environmental Protection Agency on Monday proposed to approve Alabama’s application to take over issuing certain coal waste permits from the agency, part of a trend under President Donald Trump to transfer more authority from the federal government to states.

title: First Public Fusion Firm Jumps in Wall Street Trading Debut
summary: General Fusion Group Ltd. surged in its first day of trading after merging with a blank-check firm, a sign of investors’ interest in the world’s first publicly listed nuclear fusion company.

title: Russian Billionaire Wins Fight to Boot Manager of Biotech Fund
summary: Russian billionaire Dmitry Rybolovlev’s family trust prevailed in its effort to remove the manager of a bankrupt $2.5 billion venture capital fund it backed.

title: Oil Surges as Trump Says US Wants 20% Charge for Hormuz Flows
summary: Oil surged by the most since April after US President Donald Trump said he would reimpose a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demand payment for all other cargo moving through the waterway, dashing hopes for a near-term recovery in shipping flows.

title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
summary: ホルムズ通航に20％の対価要求、近く利上げ必要に、ウォール街決算、独VWが5万人削減、円に弱気

title: Argentina IPO Pipeline Grows as YPF Power Unit Files For US Listing
summary: The power-generation unit of Argentina’s state-run energy firm YPF SA has filed for a US initial public offering, joining a growing pipeline of Argentine companies seeking New York listings.

title: Afore Banamex Chief Investment Officer Acosta to Leave Firm
summary: The chief investment officer of Mexican pension fund Afore Banamex, Tomas Acosta, is leaving the firm, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Chipotle CEO on Global Expansion, Pricing and Cyclospora Outbreak
summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill CEO Scott Boatwright discusses the company's expansion plans, pricing strategy and the latest on the cyclosporiasis outbreak on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ETF IQ 7/13/2026
summary: "Bloomberg ETF IQ" focuses on the opportunities, risks and current trends tied to the trillions of dollars in the global exchange traded funds industry. Today's guests:  Former AQR Capital Management Head of Financial Market Research &  Author of "Wrong Number" Aaron Brown, Capital Management CEO & CIO Matthew Tuttle,  JPMorgan Asset Management Global Head of ETFs Travis Spence, and Smead Capital CEO Cole Smead.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks, Bonds Fall as Oil Jump Fuels Fed-Hike Bets: Markets Wrap
summary: A standoff between the US and Iran lifted oil prices, with stocks joining bonds lower after clashes over the Strait of Hormuz raised concerns about a disruption in energy supplies that could fuel inflation.

title: Michaels CEO: Drawing Millions of Customers in Overhaul
summary: Michaels CEO David Boone joined the hosts to discuss the company's recent growth and strategic transformation. Boone addressed the challenge of changing Michaels' longstanding reputation as a 'grandmother's store' into a more contemporary and beloved brand. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Fall as Iran Blockade Restarts; Oil Surges Above $80
summary: Stocks dropped on Monday as investors assessed a tense situation in the Middle East, with President Donald Trump reinstating the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gold Drops Below $4,000 as Fed Rate Hike Bets Surge
summary: In this Metals Spotlight segment, Bloomberg's Metals Reporter Jack Ryan talks about how oil prices and a renewed US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz are impacting gold and silver prices. Bullion slid below $4,000 an ounce during trading on Monday as Fed Governor Christopher Waller said policymakers may need to raise rates in the near term. Ryan reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Hynix ADRs Tumble in Second Trading Day After Korea Selloff
summary: An AI-fueled stock rout in South Korea spilled over into the US market Monday, as SK Hynix Inc. American depositary shares fell 9.3%, underscoring growing investor concerns that the boom is overextended.

title: Strategy Raises Reserve to $3 Billion With Stock Sale
summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. raised about $467 million through the sale of common stock, lifting its cash reserve to roughly $3 billion while refraining from buying or selling Bitcoin.

title: Traders See 50% Chance That Warsh Fed Hikes Rates This Month
summary: Traders see a US interest-rate hike later this month as a coin toss, with a renewed rise in oil prices and hawkish commentary from Federal Reserve officials auguring a swift move to tame inflation.

title: Stock Rally Drives Calpers to 14.8% Return in Fiscal 2026
summary: The California Public Employees’ Retirement System reported a 14.8% gain for its latest fiscal year, with results driven by stocks and private equity.

title: Graham’s Death Deprives Ukraine of Key Trump Whisperer
summary:
The death of Senator Lindsey Graham leaves key US allies like Ukraine, which have at times struggled to influence the Trump administration, without one of their most effective conduits to the White House.
Rebeccah Heinrichs, senior fellow at Hudson Institute, joined Balance of Power and said there's momentum for Congress to pass a Russia sanctions bill that had been championed by Senator Lindsey Graham. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Slumps as US-Iran Strikes, Fed’s Waller Fuel Rate Hike Bets
summary: Gold dropped the most in more than two weeks as renewed US-Iran hostilities in the Middle East and remarks from a monetary policymaker fueled expectations that the Federal Reserve may raise interest rates to contain inflation.
